message:
fix(fs): use correct 0o777 mode for io-uring create_dir, preserve file permissions in write_atomic

Two filesystem correctness fixes:

src/fs/dir.rs — The io-uring create_dir path was using mode 0o755 instead
of 0o777 (the mode used by std::fs::create_dir / mkdir(2) before umask
application). This meant directories created via the io-uring path had
different default permissions than those created via the std path. Adds
DEFAULT_CREATE_DIR_MODE constant set to 0o777 for consistency, and a test
asserting the constant matches std::fs::create_dir semantics.

src/fs/path_ops.rs — write_atomic now preserves the existing file's Unix
permissions across the atomic rename. Before this fix, the temp file would
be created with default permissions, and the rename would swap in the new
inode losing the original file's mode bits. Now reads existing permissions
before writing, calls file.set_permissions() on the temp file before
sync_all(), then proceeds with the rename. Adds a test verifying that a
file with mode 0o700 retains its permissions after an atomic write.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
